sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: CISO Global Lands 30 Year Sales Veteran Michael Czerneda to Ignite the Next Wave of Software Growth
feed.summary: CISO Global, a provider of AI-powered cybersecurity software and compliance services, has appointed Michael Czerneda as Vice President of Sales. Czerneda, a 30-year sales veteran, will accelerate the company's transition to software-driven growth, focusing on expanding its cyber services customer base and leveraging insurance distribution channels. This strategic hire aims to build a world-class sales organization and drive adoption of CISO Global's cybersecurity software products.

feed.title: HASI prices $600 million green junior subordinated notes offering
feed.summary: HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc. (HASI) has priced a $600 million offering of 7.125% green junior subordinated notes due 2056. The company intends to use the net proceeds, estimated at $592.2 million, to repay existing credit facilities or senior notes, and to allocate an equivalent amount to eligible green projects. HASI, which has a market capitalization of approximately $4.9 billion, has a history of consistent dividend payments and projects growth in sales and net income this year.

feed.title: First National Bank of Omaha Invests $2.18 Million in Pool Corporation $POOL
feed.summary: First National Bank of Omaha has acquired a new stake of 7,014 shares in Pool Corporation (NASDAQ:POOL) during the third quarter, valued at approximately $2.18 million. Other institutional investors have also adjusted their holdings in the specialty retailer. Analyst ratings for Pool Corporation currently lean towards a "Hold" consensus with an average price target of $311.13.

Other institutional investors showed mixed activity, with some increasing and others decreasing their holdings. Analysts have a "Hold" rating on Humana, with an average price target of $228.48, following recent adjustments to price targets.

feed.title: Signet Financial Management LLC Cuts Stake in AbbVie Inc. $ABBV

Despite this, other institutional investors either bought new positions or adjusted their holdings. AbbVie (NYSE:ABBV) currently holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average target price of $251.56, and recently reported strong quarterly earnings and revenue exceeding expectations.

feed.title: Evergy Outlines 2026 Guidance and Long-Term Growth Plan
feed.summary: Evergy (EVRG) reported its full-year 2025 GAAP earnings and adjusted EPS, declaring a quarterly dividend and detailing new large load power service tariffs. The company also unveiled a $21.6 billion 2026-2030 capital plan, set 2026 adjusted EPS guidance, and outlined a 6%-8%+ long-term earnings growth target through 2030. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates EVRG as Neutral due to mixed financial performance but notes stable EPS and dividend increase.

feed.title: The Buyout Case for Salesforce Is Real, but Marc Benioff Stands in the Way
feed.summary: Salesforce stock has dropped almost 30% year-to-date, making it an attractive target for a leveraged buyout due to its strong free cash flow and undervalued stock price. However, CEO Marc Benioff's deep commitment to the company and its AI strategy, particularly Agentforce, makes a near-term sale unlikely. Investors should watch the upcoming Q4 FY2026 earnings report for potential activist pressure if results disappoint, as this could be an inflection point for the company's future.

feed.title: CenterBook Partners LP Trims Position in McKesson Corporation $MCK
feed.summary: CenterBook Partners LP reduced its stake in McKesson Corporation by 20.1% in Q3 2025, selling 1,231 shares and retaining 4,899 shares valued at approximately $3.785 million. McKesson delivered strong quarterly results, beating EPS and revenue estimates, and announced a quarterly dividend of $0.82. Despite insider share sales, the company's stock shows positive moving averages, and analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with a target price of $943.50.

feed.title: Occidental Commences Up to $700M Tender Offers for Select Notes, $58M Sub‑Cap on 2036 Zero Coupon
feed.summary: Occidental Petroleum (OXY) has initiated cash tender offers for specific senior notes and debentures, capped at an aggregate of $700 million. A sub-cap of $58 million is applied to its zero-coupon 2036 notes, which Occidental may increase. The offers, funded by cash on hand including proceeds from the recent sale of Occidental Chemical, include consent solicitations to eliminate certain covenants and have an early tender premium deadline of March 4, 2026.

feed.title: Freehand Recognized in the 2026 Gartner® Market Guide for Freight Audit and Payment Providers
feed.summary: Freehand, an agentic AI studio, has been recognized as a representative vendor in the 2026 Gartner® Market Guide for Freight Audit and Payment Providers. The company's platform utilizes agentic AI to manage and execute source-to-pay operations for supply chain teams, processing over $50 billion in global freight payments annually. This recognition highlights Freehand's role in providing modern solutions for optimizing logistics costs and driving transparency in supply chain spending.

feed.title: First National Bank of Omaha Makes New Investment in Merck & Co., Inc. $MRK
feed.summary: First National Bank of Omaha has acquired a new stake in Merck & Co., Inc. (NYSE:MRK), purchasing 32,815 shares valued at approximately $2.75 million. This investment comes as other institutional investors have also adjusted their holdings in the pharmaceutical giant. Merck recently reported strong earnings, beating analyst estimates, and announced a quarterly dividend of $0.85 per share.

feed.title: $18.67 Billion Walk-in Refrigerators Market Opportunity
feed.summary: The walk-in refrigerator market, valued at $13.03 billion in 2025, is projected to reach $18.67 billion by 2030, driven by increased foodservice infrastructure, demand for energy-efficient systems, and the expansion of retail and healthcare sectors. Key growth factors include rising restaurant numbers, more retail stores, and strategic industry acquisitions, despite challenges like shifting trade relations. Panasonic Holdings, Johnson Controls, and Blue Star are among the market leaders, with Asia-Pacific anticipated as the fastest-growing region.

feed.title: Evergy's Q4 adjusted EPS misses analyst expectations
feed.summary: Evergy (EVRG) announced that its Q4 adjusted EPS missed analyst expectations. This report, under a minute long, was provided by Refinitiv/Reuters and pertains to US stocks.

feed.title: CoStar Group Stock Experiences Significant Volatility
feed.summary: CoStar Group's stock has faced significant volatility, with its price nearly halving in the last six months due to concerns over its residential business despite a strong commercial core. The company maintains robust data network effects and high renewal rates, especially for Apartments.com. Despite an $850 million investment in Homes.com leading to losses, CoStar plans to cut spending by $300 million and initiate a $1.5 billion stock buyback, projecting a 2026 EBITDA of $770 million.

feed.title: Edwards Lifesciences CFO set for back-to-back global healthcare talks
feed.summary: Edwards Lifesciences Corporation (NYSE: EW) announced that its CFO, Scott Ullem, is scheduled to present at the Barclays 28th Annual Global Healthcare Conference on March 10, 2026, and the Leerink Global Healthcare Conference on March 11, 2026. Live webcasts of both presentations will be available on the company's investor relations website. Edwards Lifesciences is a leading global structural heart innovation company focused on improving patient lives through breakthrough technologies.

feed.title: Pool Corporation Reports Year End and Fourth Quarter 2025 Results; Provides 2026 Earnings Guidance
feed.summary: Pool Corporation (POOL) announced its full year and fourth quarter 2025 financial results, with annual net sales of $5.3 billion, consistent with 2024, and a diluted EPS of $10.85. The company also provided diluted EPS guidance for 2026 ranging from $10.85 to $11.15, anticipating modest sales growth and improved earnings. CEO Peter D. Arvan highlighted the stability of their maintenance business and growth in building materials despite ongoing consumer pressures.

feed.title: Technical Breakout Setup for Occidental Petroleum Corp (NYSE:OXY)
feed.summary: This article identifies Occidental Petroleum Corp (NYSE:OXY) as a potential technical breakout opportunity based on its strong technical health and high setup quality scores. The analysis highlights OXY's consistent uptrend, support from moving averages, and a recent sideways trading pattern indicating a potential breakout above resistance around $48.12, with a proposed stop-loss near $45.12. The report emphasizes the systematic approach to finding stocks with both solid upward momentum and low-risk entry points.

(NYSE:MET) by 5.8% in the third quarter, bringing their total holdings to 9,672,023 shares valued at approximately $796.69 million. Analysts currently have a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for MetLife with an average target price near $95.27.

feed.title: Intuit TurboTax Delivers the Ultimate “Done-For-You” Tax Experience Powered by AI and Human Intelligence With Uber Rides
feed.summary: Intuit TurboTax announced a partnership with Uber Advertising to offer free Uber rides (up to $25) to its new Expert Offices and stores, aiming to make tax preparation seamless and accessible. This initiative expands TurboTax's "Human Intelligence" service network, combining AI-powered automation for routine tasks with human expert guidance for complex financial outcomes. New or returning filers can also benefit from a flat fee of $150 for Expert Full Service (Federal and State) until March 18, 2026.

feed.title: Evergy plans $21.6B grid buildout as big power users sign on
feed.summary: Evergy (NASDAQ: EVRG) announced its 2025 financial results, with GAAP earnings of $3.66 per share and adjusted EPS of $3.83. The company declared a quarterly dividend of $0.6950 per share and established a 2026 adjusted EPS guidance of $4.14 - $4.34, alongside a long-term growth target of 6-8%+ through 2030, supported by a new $21.6 billion capital investment plan and electric service agreements with four large customers under new power service tariffs.

feed.title: C.H. Robinson Worldwide, Inc. $CHRW Shares Sold by Alberta Investment Management Corp

Despite this, institutional ownership remains high at 93.15%, with other major funds increasing their positions. C.H. Robinson recently announced a quarterly dividend and analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $181.29.

feed.title: American Airlines chooses CFM International engines to power future deliveries of Airbus A321neos
feed.summary: American Airlines has announced that CFM International's LEAP-1A engines will power its future deliveries of Airbus A321neo aircraft, reaffirming a long-standing partnership. This agreement extends the maintenance services for these engines, ensuring continued reliability and cost-efficiency. The decision supports American's fleet modernization, with 120 A321neos and 35 A321XLRs on order, all set to be equipped with CFM LEAP-1A engines.

feed.title: AI lawsuits, opt-in claims and $4B payouts reshape investor recoveries
feed.summary: Broadridge's 2026 Global Class Action Annual Report indicates that global securities class actions generated over $4 billion in investor recoveries in 2025, a decrease from $5.2 billion in 2024. The report highlights emerging trends including a rise in AI-related litigation, increased participation in opt-in and collective actions, expansion of ESG claims, and a moderation in financial antitrust activity, signaling a dynamic shift in the recovery landscape for institutional investors.

feed.title: HASI Prices $600 Million Offering of Green Junior Subordinated Notes
feed.summary: HA Sustainable Infrastructure Capital, Inc. (HASI) announced the pricing of a $600 million offering of 7.125% green junior subordinated notes due 2056. The net proceeds of approximately $592.2 million are intended to temporarily repay existing credit facilities or redeem senior notes, and will ultimately be used to acquire, invest in, or refinance eligible green projects. The offering is expected to settle on February 27, 2026, subject to customary closing conditions.

feed.title: LKQ Corporation Announces Results for Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025
feed.summary: LKQ Corporation reported its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year 2025, highlighting significant free cash flow generation despite sector headwinds. The company achieved $3.3 billion in revenue for Q4 2025 and $13.7 billion for the full year. LKQ also announced a comprehensive review of strategic alternatives to enhance shareholder value and provided its 2026 financial outlook, anticipating organic revenue growth of 0.5% to 1.5% and adjusted diluted EPS between $2.90 and $3.20.

feed.title: Quanta Services Inc. Reports Advance In Q4 Profit
feed.summary: Quanta Services Inc. (PWR) reported an increase in its Q4 profit, with earnings totaling $315.45 million, or $2.08 per share, up from $305.12 million, or $2.03 per share, last year. Excluding one-time items, the company's adjusted earnings were $479.80 million, or $3.16 per share. Revenue for the period also saw a significant rise of 19.7%, reaching $7.841 billion compared to $6.553 billion in the previous year.

feed.title: CenterPoint Energy stock falls over 2% as Q4 earnings meet expectations
feed.summary: CenterPoint Energy's stock fell over 2% in pre-market trading despite reporting fourth-quarter adjusted earnings that met Wall Street expectations at $0.45 per share. The company delivered full-year 2025 adjusted EPS of $1.76 and reiterated its 2026 guidance of $1.89-$1.91 per share, which is slightly below analyst consensus. CenterPoint also increased its 10-year capital investment plan to $65.5 billion and expects to meet its 50% increase in peak load demand by 2029, two years ahead of schedule.

feed.title: Pharmaceutical Packaging Market Trends and Growth
feed.summary: The global pharmaceutical packaging market is projected to grow from $126.2 billion in 2025 to $178.4 billion by 2030, at a CAGR of 7.2%. This growth is driven by technological advancements like smart packaging and specialized needs for biologics, as well as demand for child-resistant and senior-friendly designs. The report segments the market by material, packaging type, product, application, and region, providing detailed analysis and forecasts.

feed.title: $3.8 Billion Negative Pressure Wound Therapy (NPWT) Devices
feed.summary: The global market for Negative Pressure Wound Therapy (NPWT) devices is projected to grow from $2.9 billion in 2025 to $3.8 billion by 2030, exhibiting a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 5.9%. This growth is driven by the increasing demand for advanced wound healing technologies, particularly for managing diabetic foot ulcers and surgical site infections. The report analyzes market trends, applications, end-users, and regional dynamics, profiling major companies such as Solventum and Smith+Nephew.
